articles.title: AVOID MULTIPLE PAGE REDIRECTS: HOW REDIRECTS IMPACT PAGE SPEED
articles.summary: Website redirects send users and search engines from one URL to another, often using 3xx status codes like 301 (permanent) or 302 (temporary). While necessary for maintaining functionality after URL changes, excessive or poorly configured redirects can delay page loading, negatively impacting Core Web Vitals such as First Contentful Paint (FCP) and Largest Contentful Paint (LCP).

articles.title: A COUPLE CSS TRICKS FOR HTML DIALOG ELEMENTS
articles.summary: This article presents two CSS tricks for enhancing HTML `dialog` elements. The first trick uses the `::backdrop` pseudo-element to blur the background behind the dialog for a subtle visual effect. The second trick disables page scrolling while a dialog is open using the `body:has(dialog[open])` selector. This prevents the underlying page content from shifting during interaction with the dialog.

articles.title: HOW TO IMPROVE YOUR WFH LIGHTING TO REDUCE EYE STRAIN
articles.summary: Working from home can cause eye strain, so optimizing lighting is crucial. The ideal setup uses even, diffused light, balancing brightness to avoid both overly dim and overly bright conditions. Natural light is preferred, but artificial light should be high-quality, dimmable, and flicker-free. Strategically placed lamps can help diffuse light and minimize harsh shadows.

articles.title: THE UX OF LOGIN CODES
articles.summary: The UX of login codes often suffers from delays, confusing messaging, and annoying input fields. To make this experience better, focus on delivering codes promptly, using clear and concise messaging, and enable easy copy-paste or autofill functionality.

articles.title: HOW TO USE JOLT AI TO CONTRIBUTE NEW FEATURES TO OPEN SOURCE
articles.summary: Jolt is AI for 100K+ codebases. It accurately selects context files in multi-million line codebases and generates multi-file changes while matching your code style. In this video [9], you can see how Jolt tackles a GitHub issue about adding standard-deviation shading

articles.title: (AreaRange) to Victory Native line charts. Watch the video
TAILWIND CSS V4.0
articles.summary: Tailwind CSS v4.0 is a major release that focuses on performance improvements, with up to 5x faster full builds and over 100x faster incremental builds. The framework uses modern CSS features like cascade layers and custom properties for more flexibility. Some changes include a new CSS-first configuration, the replacement of the JavaScript config file for customization, and the introduction of new utilities for 3D transforms, gradients, and container queries.

articles.title: BROWSERAI
articles.summary: BrowserAI is an open-source project that allows you to run LLMs directly in your web browser. It provides offline capabilities, privacy, and speed with WebGPU acceleration.

articles.title: REACT NATIVE 0.77 IS NOW AVAILABLE WITH EXPO SDK 52
articles.summary: React Native 0.77 has been released. Expo SDK 52 supports both 0.76 and 0.77. React Native 0.77 has new styling capabilities (like `display: contents` and `boxSizing`), Android 16KB page support, and a Swift-based community template (while maintaining Objective-C compatibility).

articles.title: A $1.8B STARTUP SALE MADE HIM WEALTHY - NOW HE PLANS TO DONATE HALF HIS NET WORTH: 'THE AMERICAN DREAM ISN'T JUST ABOUT GETTING RICH'
articles.summary: Jeff Atwood, co-founder of Stack Overflow, plans to donate over half his net worth within five years following the $1.8 billion sale of his company. He's concerned about wealth inequality and limited opportunities for many Americans, so he aims to address these issues through philanthropy.

articles.title: MASTERCARD DNS ERROR WENT UNNOTICED FOR YEARS
articles.summary: A five-year-old typo in MasterCard's DNS settings allowed potential interception of internet traffic. Security researcher Philippe Caturegli discovered and registered the misconfigured domain "akam.ne" for $300, preventing potential exploitation. MasterCard acknowledged the error and stated there was no actual risk to its systems, but Caturegli disputes this claim.

articles.title: THE 7 MOST INFLUENTIAL PAPERS IN COMPUTER SCIENCE HISTORY
articles.summary: This is a subjective list of seven highly influential papers in computer science history. The list spans from Alan Turing's foundational work on computation in 1936 to Sergey Brin and Larry Page's development of PageRank in 1998. The papers cover key areas like information theory, database management, algorithm complexity, and the architecture of the internet.

articles.title: MASTER THE ART OF THE PRODUCT MANAGER 'NO'
articles.summary: Creative ways to say “no” in nice ways, like “let's circle back after we've tackled the core priorities.”

articles.title: ASK HN: IS ANYONE DOING ANYTHING COOL WITH TINY LANGUAGE MODELS? (HACKER NEWS THREAD)
articles.summary: This Hacker News thread discusses various creative applications of small (0.5B-3B parameter) language models, including an Excel add-in for filtering research papers, a tool for identifying cookie notices, and a system for responding to SMS spam.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY LAUNCHES SONAR, AN API FOR AI SEARCH
articles.summary: Perplexity has launched Sonar, an API that allows enterprises and developers to integrate its generative AI search tools into applications, providing real-time, citation-backed answers.

articles.title: TOP PENS OF 2024 ON CODEPEN
articles.summary: CodePen showcases the top-rated code projects of 2024.
